title: Fed holds rates steady but dot plot signals one more cut this year
summary: FOMC statement kept the Fed funds rate at 4.25-4.50%. Powell told reporters the committee is watching core CPI and jobs data. The updated dot plot shows a median of one more 25bp cut before year-end.

title: S&P 500 edges to fresh record as bank earnings roll in
summary: JPMorgan and Wells Fargo both beat on top and bottom lines. Wall Street analysts now expect the S&P 500 to close above 6100 by mid-year. NASDAQ up 0.7%, led by chips.

title: Bitcoin breaks $95k after ETF inflows hit weekly high
summary: BTC crossed $95,000 in overnight trade. Spot bitcoin ETFs saw $1.8B in weekly inflows, the biggest since launch. Ethereum also up, trading above $4,200.

title: Stablecoin market cap crosses $300B as USDC resumes growth
summary: Circle's USDC grew 4% in the last month after the MiCA-compliant issuer re-entered European markets. DeFi TVL climbed back above $120B for the first time since 2022.

title: CPI print lands at 2.3% year-over-year, below consensus
summary: March CPI came in softer than expected. Core inflation dropped to 2.6%. Traders now price in 72% probability of a June Fed cut, up from 58% yesterday.

title: Jobs report: 186k nonfarm payrolls, unemployment steady at 4.1%
summary: Payrolls came in below the 210k expectation. Wage growth slowed to 3.7% YoY. Services sector added most of the gains; manufacturing shed 11k jobs.

title: Anthropic ships Claude 4.7 with 1M-token context and built-in tool search
summary: The new model doubles the context window and adds native tool-search so agents no longer need custom routing. Early benchmarks show 18% improvement on long-context reasoning over Claude 4.6.

title: OpenAI open-sources GPT-5 Mini under Apache 2.0
summary: OpenAI released a 7B-parameter version of GPT-5 Mini as an open-weights model. The license allows commercial use. This is the first open OpenAI model since GPT-2.

title: Cursor raises $400M Series C at $9B valuation led by Thrive
summary: The AI code editor passed $500M ARR and now employs 240. Thrive Capital led with participation from Sequoia and a16z. Cursor says it will invest in model fine-tuning infrastructure.

title: GitHub ships Agent Mode by default for all Copilot users
summary: Agent Mode turns Copilot into an autonomous editor that can plan, edit, and test across files. Rolling out to 20M Copilot seats this week. Comes with tool-use sandboxing.

title: Anthropic and DoD sign $200M defense agent contract
summary: The Pentagon awarded Anthropic a two-year contract to build classified-environment agents for logistics planning. Claude will be fine-tuned on internal DoD document formats.

title: Arxiv paper: Sparse-MoE transformers hit GPT-4 quality at 1/8 inference cost
summary: A new paper from DeepMind claims their 34B-active-param Sparse-MoE transformer matches GPT-4 on MMLU while running 8x cheaper at inference. Code and weights released on HuggingFace.

title: Steam breaks 40M concurrent users for the first time
summary: Valve's platform hit 40.2M simultaneous players on Saturday, a new all-time high. The surge was driven by the launch of Path of Exile 2 and a deep-discount weekend sale.

title: Nintendo teases Switch 3 for 2027 holiday window
summary: In a quarterly investor call Nintendo confirmed a successor to the Switch 2 is in development. The company stopped short of a formal reveal but said the new console will be backwards compatible.

title: Esports viewership hits 680M globally in Q1
summary: League of Legends, Valorant, and Counter-Strike 2 drove most of the growth. The top event of the quarter was the LCK Spring Final, peaking at 4.1M concurrent viewers.

title: Bluesky crosses 40M users as Threads passes 300M MAU
summary: Bluesky's user base tripled in the last six months. Meta's Threads crossed 300M monthly actives, closing in on X's reported numbers. TikTok is still the most-used social platform in the US under-30 cohort.

title: Senate passes stopgap funding bill in 68-30 vote
summary: Congress averted a government shutdown with a continuing resolution that funds federal agencies through July. The bill heads to the president's desk. A longer-term FY26 budget fight is still pending.

title: Supreme Court hears arguments in AI copyright case
summary: Justices probed both sides of Authors Guild v. OpenAI, the most-watched AI case of the term. A decision is expected in June. The DOJ filed an amicus brief siding with the authors.

title: EU finalizes AI Act enforcement rules for general-purpose models
summary: The European Commission published the implementing regulation for GPAI model providers. Companies above 10^25 FLOPs of training compute must register with the EU AI Office by June 1.

title: NBA playoffs: Celtics and Thunder take 2-0 series leads
summary: Jayson Tatum put up 38 in Boston's Game 2 win over the Cavs. In the West, Oklahoma City's Shai Gilgeous-Alexander had 34 in a 112-99 win over the Nuggets.

title: NFL Draft first round: QB run in top 10
summary: Three quarterbacks went in the first six picks. The Giants traded up to grab the consensus best QB at pick 3. Full first round highlighted by a run on offensive linemen.

title: Premier League: Arsenal clinch Champions League spot
summary: Arsenal's 3-1 win at Villa Park guarantees top-four finish. Manchester City and Liverpool are still battling for the title with three matches remaining.

title: Balenciaga names new creative director ahead of Paris Fashion Week
summary: The LVMH-owned house tapped a 34-year-old Belgian designer for its creative lead role. Her debut collection drops in September. Fashion critics call it the boldest succession in recent memory.

title: Met Gala 2026: theme revealed as 'Silver Age of Cinema'
summary: Anna Wintour announced the theme on Instagram. The exhibit at the Costume Institute pairs 1930s Hollywood couture with contemporary haute couture reinterpretations.

title: Taylor Swift announces surprise acoustic album
summary: The 11-track record was produced in two weeks and will drop next Friday. First single is already topping Spotify's global chart.

title: Christopher Nolan's next film casts Zendaya and Cillian Murphy
summary: Universal confirmed the untitled project is a near-future sci-fi set in Mumbai. Shooting starts in September; theatrical release pegged for July 2027. IMAX cameras only.

title: Netflix picks up 'The Archive' from David Fincher for 2027
summary: The 10-episode series is based on a forthcoming novel by Emily St. John Mandel. Fincher directs the first two episodes. Filming begins in Toronto this summer.

title: Pulitzer fiction winner is a 320-page novel about a climate scientist
summary: The committee praised the book's structural invention and 'restraint in the face of apocalypse'. The winning author is a debut novelist and a former ecology researcher.

title: Hollywood couple announces surprise engagement at Cannes
summary: Two A-list actors confirmed the engagement on Instagram during the Cannes festival. Sources say they met on a film set last year. The wedding is reportedly set for Greece.

title: FDA approves first oral weight-loss drug for teens
summary: The pill version of semaglutide received FDA approval for use in adolescents aged 12-17 with clinical obesity. The decision caps a two-year review; insurance coverage questions remain open.

title: WHO declares end to latest H5N1 outbreak
summary: No new human cases in 60 days across the monitored regions. Officials credit fast poultry-sector coordination and the new rapid-response vaccine stockpile.

title: Boston Marathon: course records broken in both men's and women's fields
summary: The elite field saw two sub-2:03 men's finishes and a 2:14:50 women's winner. Weather was near-perfect. Training philosophy around 'super shoes' is now mainstream.

title: Michelin Guide returns to Seoul with three new three-stars
summary: Two fine-dining restaurants and a kaiseki-Korean fusion house earned three stars. The Seoul guide now lists 189 starred restaurants total, up from 136 last year.

title: Airlines warn of summer capacity crunch as air traffic controller shortage persists
summary: The FAA is short 3,400 controllers. United, Delta, and American have trimmed summer schedules. Fares to European destinations are already running 8% above last year.

title: SpaceX Starship completes first fully-reusable orbital flight
summary: Both the booster and the upper stage returned to catch-arms at Starbase. Payload was a batch of next-gen Starlink satellites. Musk said a Mars payload demo is next.

title: JWST paper: water vapor detected in atmosphere of K2-18b
summary: A peer-reviewed study in Nature confirms the water signature earlier hinted at by preliminary JWST transit data. The sub-Neptune exoplanet is now the top target for follow-up observations.

title: Carbon removal hits 100k tons delivered globally for the first time
summary: CDR buyers have now received 100,000 verified tons of removed carbon across direct air capture, enhanced weathering, and biomass carbon removal. Price per ton averaging $480.

title: China's solar capacity now exceeds its coal capacity
summary: The National Energy Administration confirmed installed solar capacity passed 1,200 GW, overtaking coal for the first time. EV sales in-country also hit a record 1.1M units last month.

title: Tech layoffs slow to lowest monthly total in three years
summary: March saw 8,400 tech layoffs, the smallest figure since early 2023. Hiring has picked up for ML engineers and distributed systems roles.

title: Mortgage rates dip to 6.1% as housing market finds footing
summary: The 30-year fixed mortgage average fell 12 basis points in a single week. Existing-home sales ticked up 2.1% month-over-month. Rent growth cooled to 2.9% YoY.

title: Student loan forgiveness program clears DOJ review, set to disburse
summary: A narrower version of the 2024 forgiveness plan survived court review. 940,000 borrowers will see balances reduced. Disbursements begin in May.
